name: Compromised Mailbox Rule Audit
description: Audits and remediates malicious email forwarding rules created by attackers after a successful phishing compromise. Queries the compromised user's mailbox rules via Microsoft Graph, identifies suspicious forwarding or auto-delete rules, removes malicious rules, and notifies the user and SOC. Attackers commonly create hidden forwarding rules to maintain persistent access to email communications. This playbook ensures those rules are detected and removed as part of incident response.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: Microsoft Graph
required_integrations.reason: Query and remove mailbox rules from the compromised account

required_integrations.name: Slack
required_integrations.reason: Notify SOC about discovered malicious mailbox rules

data.title: Mailbox Compromise Alert
data.summary: Triggers on compromised mailbox or suspicious forwarding rule alerts

data.title: Query Mailbox Rules
data.summary: Retrieve all inbox rules from the compromised user's mailbox

data.title: Identify Suspicious Rules
data.summary: Analyze mailbox rules to find forwarding, redirect, or auto-delete rules

script.code:
def main(rules, alert):
    all_rules = rules.get('rules', rules.get('value', []))
    suspicious = []
    clean = []
    for rule in all_rules:
        actions = rule.get('actions', {})
        is_suspicious = False
        reasons = []
        fwd = actions.get('forwardTo', actions.get('forward_to', []))
        redir = actions.get('redirectTo', actions.get('redirect_to', []))
        delete = actions.get('delete', actions.get('permanentDelete', False))
        move_to = actions.get('moveToFolder', '')
        if fwd:
            is_suspicious = True
            reasons.append(f'Forwards to: {fwd}')
        if redir:
            is_suspicious = True
            reasons.append(f'Redirects to: {redir}')
        if delete:
            is_suspicious = True
            reasons.append('Auto-deletes messages')
        if move_to and 'deleted' in str(move_to).lower():
            is_suspicious = True
            reasons.append(f'Moves to: {move_to}')
        entry = {
            'rule_id': rule.get('id', ''),
            'name': rule.get('displayName', rule.get('name', 'Unnamed')),
            'enabled': rule.get('isEnabled', True),
            'reasons': reasons
        }
        if is_suspicious:
            suspicious.append(entry)
        else:
            clean.append(entry)
    return {
        'suspicious_rules': suspicious,
        'clean_rules': clean,
        'suspicious_count': len(suspicious),
        'total_rules': len(all_rules),
        'affected_user': alert.get('affected_user', '')
    }

data.title: Suspicious Rules Found?
data.summary: Were any suspicious forwarding or auto-delete rules detected?

data.title: For Each Suspicious Rule
data.summary: Iterate over each suspicious mailbox rule for removal

data.title: Remove Malicious Rule
data.summary: Delete the suspicious mailbox rule via Microsoft Graph

data.title: Notify SOC & User
data.summary: Alert SOC and notify the affected user about removed rules

data.title: Close — No Suspicious Rules
data.summary: No suspicious mailbox rules found, close alert

data.title: End — Rules Removed
data.summary: Malicious mailbox rules removed, user and SOC notified

config.summary: Malicious mailbox rules detected and removed. SOC and user notified. Password reset recommended.
Mailbox rule audit completed. No suspicious rules found. Alert closed.
